questions.question: Identify the next even number in the pattern: $2, 4, 6, 8, 10$
A basketball player scored $-3$ points in a game due to penalties. If they scored $5$ points from shots, what is their total score?
A gymnast performs $15$ routines. If $9$ routines are odd-numbered, how many are even-numbered?
The temperature at a sports stadium is $-2$ degrees Celsius. If it rises by $5$ degrees, what is the new temperature?
A cyclist travels $12$ km in $2$ hours. How many kilometers do they travel per hour?
A sports team wins $8$ games and loses $-2$ games. What is their total number of games played?
Identify the pattern in the sequence: $1, 3, 5, 7, 9$
A marathon runner travels $25$ km in $5$ hours. If they travel at a constant speed, how many kilometers will they travel in $8$ hours? Show your calculations and explain your reasoning.

A swimmer finishes a race in $2$ minutes and $15$ seconds. If their opponent finished the same race in $2$ minutes and $30$ seconds, how many seconds faster did the swimmer finish? Convert all times to seconds and show your work.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of showing work and calculations for free-response questions
Encourage students to use real-world examples to illustrate their understanding of natural numbers and integers
Remind students to check their units and ensure they are answering the question being asked
